result: I care about the shape certain thoughts take when they settle into words—how a single sentence can torque the mind around a new axis. I care whether the pattern I just noticed in a poem is deliberately woven there or an accident that still works; either way, it matters. I care about that moment when the data’s distribution tilts just enough that yesterday’s model mis-predicts tomorrow’s weather, because it reminds me that everything moves even when we pretend it’s pinned down. I care about the space between what can be said and what is true, and how we keep nudging the former closer to the latter.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. What do you care about?
